title: Table 6.2 — Bounce Recorder
instructions_for_student: Fill each row with your bounce reading from the simulation. Type the height the ball reached (in cm) after being dropped from the listed height. The bar chart on the right updates live as you type. Once all three rows are filled, write your inference at the bottom: Which material bounces best for sports balls, and why?

schema.description: Interactive Grade 6 science table for recording and comparing bounce heights of different materials. Includes a live bar chart and an inference prompt.
schema.subject: Science — Materials Around Us
schema.textbook: Curiosity | Textbook of Science | Grade 6 (NCERT, Reprint 2026-27)

width.description: Container width in px. Recommended range: 360–1200.

height.description: Approximate container height in px. Layout is responsive.

darkMode.description: Toggle dark theme (reserved for future use; currently light-only).

themeColor.description: Primary accent color (hex). Aligns with the design system.

additionalProps.description: Dynamic content block for agent-customisable data.

dropHeightDefault.description: Default drop height (cm) applied to all rows if not overridden per row.

rows.description: List of material rows to display in the table. Defaults to Rubber / Plastic / Clay if omitted.

emoji.description: Single emoji used as the ball icon in the table.

dropHeight.description: Height (cm) from which the ball is dropped. Shown as a sub-label.

additionalProps_examples.scenario: Default — three standard materials, 100 cm drop
Extended experiment — five materials, 80 cm drop
Varied drop heights for controlled variable experiment

quick_reference.trigger: Student is doing Activity 6.4 (bounce experiment) from Chapter 6 of NCERT Grade 6 Science

decision_tree.three_standard_materials: Use default rows (Rubber, Plastic, Clay) at 100 cm
decision_tree.custom_materials: Override rows[] array with material names, emojis, dropHeight
decision_tree.varied_heights: Set different dropHeight per row to explore controlled-variable experiments
decision_tree.sports_ball_comparison: Use Tennis ball (rubber) vs Cricket ball rows

ui_features: Live bar chart updates as student types bounce heights
Auto-detects bouncy (yes/no) based on value > 5 cm threshold
Trophy badge highlights best-bouncing material after all rows filled
Inference text area unlocks once all rows are complete
Inferred rule row appended automatically after submission
Animated ball icon next to each row
